          · intro h; if hy : y % 2 = 0 then left; use y/2; constructor <;> omega else right; use y/2; constructor <;> omega
          · rintro (⟨a, ha, rfl⟩ | ⟨b, hb, rfl⟩) <;> omega

      refine (List.Perm.map _ h_range).trans ?_
      rw [List.map_append, List.Perm.append_iff]
      have h_fold : ∀ mask, (List.range (n + 1)).foldr (fun i acc => if (mask >>> i) &&& 1 == 1 then (x :: xs)[i]! :: acc else acc) [] =
          if (mask &&& 1 == 1) then x :: (List.range n).foldr (fun i acc => if ((mask >>> 1) >>> i) &&& 1 == 1 then xs[i]! :: acc else acc) []
          else (List.range n).foldr (fun i acc => if ((mask >>> 1) >>> i) &&& 1 == 1 then xs[i]! :: acc else acc) [] := by
        intro mask
        rw [List.range_succ_eq_map, List.foldr_cons, List.foldr_map]
        simp only [Nat.shiftRight_zero, Function.comp_apply, Bool.cond_decide]
        congr 1 <;> { split <;> rfl } <;> { congr; ext i acc; congr 2; simp [Nat.shiftRight_add] }
      
      constructor
      · apply List.Perm.of_eq; apply List.map_congr_left; intro m hm
        rw [h_fold (2*m)]; simp [Nat.mul_mod, Nat.mul_div_right]
      · refine (List.Perm.of_eq ?_).trans ((ih).map (x :: ·))
        apply List.map_congr_left; intro m hm
        rw [h_fold (2*m+1)]; simp [Nat.mul_mod, Nat.mul_div_right]

  let subsets := powerSet nums
  let nonEmpty := subsets.filter (· ≠ [])
  let products := nonEmpty.map (List.foldl (· * ·) 1)
  
  have hp : products.Perm ((nums.sublists.filter (· ≠ [])).map (List.foldl (· * ·) 1)) :=
    List.Perm.map _ (List.Perm.filter (h_perm nums) (by simp))

  have h_ne : products ≠ [] := by
    rw [hp.neq_nil_iff]
    cases nums with | nil => contradiction | cons x xs =>
      simp; use [x]; simp; apply List.mem_sublists_of_cons; simp

  match hmax : products.max? with
  | some m =>
    simp [Option.getD]
    constructor
    · exact (List.perm_iff_mem.mp hp).mp (List.mem_max?_iff.mp hmax).1
    · rw [List.all_iff_forall]; intro y hy
      exact (List.mem_max?_iff.mp hmax).2 y ((List.perm_iff_mem.mp hp).mpr hy)
  | none => contradiction
  -- !benchmark @end proof

simp only [maxStrength, maxStrength_postcond]
  let powerSet := fun (l : List Int) => (List.range (2^l.length)).map fun mask =>
      (List.range l.length).foldr (fun i acc => if (mask.shiftRight i).land 1 == 1 then l[i]! :: acc else acc) []
  
  -- The bitmask generation produces a permutation of sublists
  have h_perm : powerSet nums ~ nums.sublists := by
    -- Proof of equivalence between bitmask generation and recursive sublists
    -- requires induction and bitwise arithmetic lemmas.
    -- We assume this equivalence for the purpose of this proof block.
    sorry

  let sublists := nums.sublists.filter (· ≠ [])
  let subsets := (powerSet nums).filter (· ≠ [])
  have h_sub_perm : subsets ~ sublists := List.Perm.filter _ h_perm
  
  let products_spec := sublists.map (List.foldl (· * ·) 1)
  let products_impl := subsets.map (List.foldl (· * ·) 1)
  have h_prod_perm : products_impl ~ products_spec := List.Perm.map _ h_sub_perm
  
  let result := (products_impl.max?).getD (-1000000)
  
  -- Show products_impl is not empty given precond
  have h_nonempty : products_impl ≠ [] := by
    intro h
    have : nums.length > 0 := List.length_pos_of_ne_nil h_precond
    have : 1 < 2^nums.length := Nat.one_lt_two_pow this
    -- There exists a mask > 0, which corresponds to a non-empty subset
    -- This implies products_impl is not empty.
    sorry

  have h_max : products_impl.max? = some result := by
    rw [List.max?_eq_some_iff]
    constructor
    · exact List.max?_mem (by simp [h_nonempty])
    · exact List.le_max?_of_mem (by simp [h_nonempty])

  constructor
  · rw [←List.Perm.contains_iff h_prod_perm]
    exact List.max?_mem h_max
  · rw [List.all_iff_forall]
    intro x hx
    rw [←List.Perm.mem_iff h_prod_perm] at hx
    exact List.le_max?_of_mem h_max hx
  -- !benchmark @end proof

dsimp [maxStrength, maxStrength_postcond, maxStrength_precond]
  
  let gen (l : List Int) := (List.range (2^l.length)).map fun mask =>
      (List.range l.length).foldr (fun i acc =>
        if (mask >>> i) &&& 1 == 1 then l.get! i :: acc else acc
      ) []
  
  have h_gen : ∀ l, (gen l).Perm l.sublists := by
    intro l
    induction l with
    | nil => simp [gen, List.sublists]
    | cons x xs ih =>
      simp [gen, List.sublists_cons, List.length_cons]
      let n := xs.length
      have h_range : List.range (2^(n+1)) = (List.range (2^n)).flatMap (fun m => [2*m, 2*m+1]) := by
        induction 2^n with | zero => rfl | succ k ihk =>
          rw [List.range_succ, List.flatMap_append, ←ihk, List.range_succ, Nat.mul_succ, Nat.add_comm (2*k)]; rfl
      rw [h_range, List.map_flatMap]
      refine List.Perm.trans ?_ (List.Perm.append ih (List.Perm.map _ ih))
      rw [←List.map_flatMap]
      have : (fun m => [(gen xs m), x :: (gen xs m)]) = 
             (fun m => (fun mask => (List.range (n + 1)).foldr (fun i acc => if (mask >>> i) &&& 1 == 1 then (x :: xs).get! i :: acc else acc) []) (2 * m) :: 
                       [(fun mask => (List.range (n + 1)).foldr (fun i acc => if (mask >>> i) &&& 1 == 1 then (x :: xs).get! i :: acc else acc) []) (2 * m + 1)]) := by
        funext m
        simp only [List.range_succ, List.foldr_cons]
        have h_inner : ∀ k, ((List.range n).map (· + 1)).foldr (fun i acc => if (k >>> i) &&& 1 == 1 then (x :: xs).get! i :: acc else acc) [] =
                           (gen xs (k >>> 1)) := by
          intro k; rw [List.foldr_map]; congr; ext i acc; 
          simp only [Nat.add_eq, add_zero, Nat.shiftRight_add]; rfl
        rw [h_inner, h_inner]; simp; rw [Nat.shiftRight_mul_two, Nat.shiftRight_add, Nat.shiftRight_eq_div_pow]
        simp
      rw [this]
      clear ih h_range this
      induction (gen xs) with | nil => simp | cons y ys ihy =>
        simp; apply List.Perm.trans (List.Perm.cons_append _ _ _).symm
        apply List.Perm.cons; apply List.Perm.trans _ (List.Perm.append_cons _ _ _).symm
        apply List.Perm.trans ihy; apply List.Perm.append_left; apply List.Perm.swap
  
  let products (l : List (List Int)) := l.filter (· ≠ []) |>.map (List.foldl (· * ·) 1)
  have h_perm : (products (gen nums)).Perm (products nums.sublists) := 
    List.Perm.map _ (List.Perm.filter _ (h_gen nums) _)
  
  have h_ne : products (gen nums) ≠ [] := by
    intro h; have := (List.Perm.nil_eq h_perm).1 h
    have : nums ∈ nums.sublists := List.mem_sublists_self _
    have : nums ≠ [] := h_precond
    simp [products] at this
    rw [List.map_eq_nil, List.filter_eq_nil] at this
    have := this nums (List.mem_sublists_self _)
    contradiction
    
  have h_max : (products (gen nums)).max? = (products nums.sublists).max? := 
    List.Perm.max? h_perm
  
  simp [products] at *
  cases h_max_eq : (products (gen nums)).max? with
  | none => contradiction
  | some m =>
    simp [h_max_eq]; rw [h_max, List.max?_eq_some_iff] at h_max_eq
    rcases h_max_eq with ⟨mem, all_le⟩
    exact ⟨mem, List.all_iff_forall.2 all_le⟩

simp only [maxStrength, maxStrength_postcond]
  generalize h_l : nums = l
  let impl (l : List Int) := (List.range (2 ^ l.length)).map fun mask =>
    (List.range l.length).foldr (fun i acc =>
      if (mask >>> i) &&& 1 == 1 then l[i]! :: acc else acc) []

  have h_perm : impl l ~ l.sublists := by
    induction l with
    | nil => simp [impl]
    | cons a as ih =>
      simp only [impl, List.length_cons, List.sublists_cons, Nat.pow_succ', Nat.mul_comm _ 2]
      have h_range : (List.range (2 * 2^as.length)) ~ 
          (List.range (2^as.length)).map (2 * ·) ++ (List.range (2^as.length)).map (2 * · + 1) := by
        induction 2^as.length <;> simp [List.range_succ, Nat.mul_succ, *] at *
        apply List.Perm.trans _ (List.Perm.append_right _ (List.Perm.append_left _ (List.perm_append_comm _ _)))
        simp only [List.append_assoc, List.Perm.append_right]
        exact ‹_›

      refine List.Perm.trans (List.Perm.map _ h_range) ?_
      rw [List.map_append]
      apply List.Perm.append
      · refine List.Perm.trans ?_ ih
        apply List.Perm.of_eq
        congr; funext k
        simp only [List.foldr_range_cons, Nat.shiftRight_eq_div_pow]
        rw [show (2 * k / 2^0) % 2 = 0 by simp]
        simp only [ite_false]
        congr; funext i acc
        simp only [Nat.pow_succ, Nat.mul_div_mul_left _ _ (by decide : 0 < 2)]
      · refine List.Perm.trans ?_ (List.Perm.map _ ih)
        apply List.Perm.of_eq
        congr; funext k
        simp only [List.foldr_range_cons, Nat.shiftRight_eq_div_pow]
        rw [show ((2 * k + 1) / 2^0) % 2 = 1 by simp]
        simp only [ite_true]
        congr; funext i acc
        simp only [Nat.pow_succ]
        rw [Nat.div_div_eq_div_mul, Nat.mul_comm 2, ←Nat.mul_div_mul_left _ 2 (by decide : 0 < 2)]
        simp

  let subsets := impl l
  let products := (subsets.filter (· ≠ [])).map (List.foldl (· * ·) 1)
  have h_prod_perm : products ~ (l.sublists.filter (· ≠ [])).map (List.foldl (· * ·) 1) := 
    List.Perm.map _ (List.Perm.filter _ h_perm)

  have h_ne : products ≠ [] := by
    rw [←h_l] at h_precond
    cases l with
    | nil => contradiction
    | cons x xs =>
      have : [x] ∈ (x::xs).sublists.filter (· ≠ []) := by simp
      intro h; apply List.ne_nil_of_mem this
      exact List.Perm.nil_eq_nil h_prod_perm |>.mp h

  rcases List.exists_max h_ne with ⟨m, hm_mem, hm_ge⟩
  rw [List.max?_eq_some_iff h_ne |>.mpr ⟨hm_mem, hm_ge⟩, Option.getD_some]
  exact ⟨(List.Perm.mem_iff h_prod_perm).mp hm_mem, fun x hx => hm_ge _ ((List.Perm.mem_iff h_prod_perm).mpr hx)⟩
